text.title: PM Tool Quick-Reference Profiles
text.summary: Comparative profiles of 10 major PM tools with strengths, weaknesses, and team-size fit.

section.title: Quick Reference
section.p: Tool fit is determined by team size, workflow complexity, ecosystem integration, and compliance posture — not feature counts. "Best for X" labels are directional; always validate against the team's actual MoSCoW requirements.

list.item: Jira: enterprise, compliance-heavy, 50+ users. Highly customizable; complex; large ecosystem.
Linear: engineering-focused, 10-100 users. Fastest UX; keyboard-first; opinionated workflow; GitHub-native.
ClickUp: all-in-one consolidation, 10-500 users. Most features; some performance issues; steep learning curve.
Notion: docs + light PM, 5-100 users. Flexible; beautiful; not PM-primary; weak reporting.
GitHub Projects (v2): developers already in GitHub, any size. Free for OSS; GraphQL; limited PM depth.
GitLab Boards: DevOps-integrated teams, 10-1000 users. Full DevOps platform; Premium for WIP limits.
Azure DevOps: Microsoft-stack enterprises, 50+ users. Compliance-grade; Entra integration; CMMI support.
Trello: simple Kanban, 5-50 users. Easy onboarding; limited scaling; good for low-complexity workflows.
Asana: marketing and ops teams, 10-500 users. Clean UX; goals and portfolios; less dev-focused.
Monday.com: non-technical teams, 10-500 users. Visual; highly flexible; expensive at scale.

rule.statement: Choose Jira for organizations with 50+ users, complex approval workflows, compliance requirements (SOC2, FedRAMP), or need for extensive plugin ecosystem.
rule.rationale: Jira's value is in customizability and ecosystem breadth. For simpler needs, the UI complexity and performance overhead outweigh the benefits.

antipattern.description: Using Jira for a team of fewer than 10 with straightforward Kanban needs.
antipattern.reason: Jira's configuration overhead (workflows, schemes, custom fields) creates administrative burden that kills small-team productivity.

rule.statement: Choose Linear for developer-focused teams of 10-100 that prioritize speed, keyboard-driven UX, and tight GitHub integration over flexibility.
rule.rationale: Linear's opinionated design eliminates configuration decisions, which accelerates adoption and daily use for engineering teams.

antipattern.description: Choosing Linear when stakeholders need heavy customization, large ecosystem apps, or mature reporting.
antipattern.reason: Linear's opinionated design is a liability when teams need workflow customization that Linear does not support out of the box.

list.item: Jira: REST v3 + GraphQL alpha; full CRUD; JQL for queries. Best for complex query-driven workflows.
Linear: GraphQL-first; fast; excellent pagination. Best for engineering-metric automation.
GitHub Projects v2: GraphQL; free; tightly coupled to repo events. Best for code-adjacent workflows.
GitLab: REST + GraphQL; self-host option; webhook-driven. Best for DevOps-integrated automation.
Azure DevOps: REST + OData Analytics; enterprise auth. Best for Microsoft-stack traceability pipelines.
